task:
Atom 0 — Launch housekeeping: archive the met predecessor priority `orchestration-pipeline-simplification` following the LIVE archive convention. This unblocks open ticket 0023 (it could not be archived post-wrap in run_165). Governance/docs only.

WHAT TO DO (follow the archive-priority Play convention exactly — `packages/personas/base/plays/archive-priority.md`):
1. Confirm the live archive convention is the directory `cocoder/priorities/archive/<id>.md` by observing the existing archive dir (it already holds many `<id>.md` files, e.g. `founder-brief-format-durability.md`). Use that convention; do NOT invent a second mechanism and do NOT use `cocoder/zArchive/`.
2. Move `cocoder/priorities/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md` to `cocoder/priorities/archive/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md`. Preserve the file contents verbatim (this is a move, not a rewrite) — a fresh agent must still be able to read why it was archived and what its dispositions were.
3. `cocoder/priorities/order.json` does NOT currently list `orchestration-pipeline-simplification` (verify this — current ids are orchestration-audit-and-refactor, deb-follows-oscar, drift-audit, new-primary-root, first-class-model-tiers, adapter-abstraction-hardening, tickets-review, priority-audit). Because it is absent, leave `order.json` byte-for-byte unchanged. If you find it present, remove only that id and preserve JSON shape and every other id.
4. Close open ticket 0023 if the repo convention is to close a ticket whose defect this atom resolves — but ONLY if you can confirm the ticket-close convention (e.g. move `cocoder/tickets/open/0023-*.md` to `cocoder/tickets/closed/` and update `cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md`) by reading how other closed tickets were handled. If the close convention is not obvious from the existing tree, DO NOT guess — leave ticket 0023 open and note in your completion summary that closing it is a follow-up.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `cocoder/priorities/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md` no longer exists at the old path and exists identically under `cocoder/priorities/archive/`.
- `order.json` remains valid JSON and (given the id is absent) is unchanged.
- The archived file still parses as a priority (frontmatter intact).
- No other priority files touched.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not edit any other priority, do not touch `cocoder/zArchive/`, do not run git or commit (the runner commits on my verify pass). Report the exact paths moved and whether you touched ticket 0023.

WRITE SCOPE: cocoder/priorities/**, cocoder/tickets/** (the latter only if you confirm the close convention).

Atom 0 (corrected re-delegation) — Launch housekeeping: archive the met predecessor priority ONLY. Your previous attempt had the archive move correct but also CLOSED ticket 0023, which is wrong and was rejected at verify. Ticket 0023 is an unfixed CODE defect (the archive-priority Play has no out-of-run dispatch surface; its acceptance needs a reachable CLI/surface + a one-owner enforcer — a raw file move is the workaround, not the fix). It must stay OPEN.

NOTE on tree state: your previous (uncommitted) changes are still in the working tree. Keep the good part, undo the bad part.

WHAT TO DO:
1. KEEP the archive move: `cocoder/priorities/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md` stays moved to `cocoder/priorities/archive/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md`, contents byte-identical to the original (it was verified identical — do not rewrite it).
2. KEEP `cocoder/priorities/order.json` unchanged (the id was never listed — correct).
3. UNDO the ticket 0023 closure completely, restoring it to its committed (HEAD) state:
   - Move `cocoder/tickets/closed/0023-archive-priority-play-no-out-of-run-dispatch.md` back to `cocoder/tickets/open/0023-archive-priority-play-no-out-of-run-dispatch.md`.
   - Restore its frontmatter to exactly HEAD: `status: Open`, remove the `closed: 2026-06-20` line, and remove the appended "Closed by run_166 atom 0..." closure note at the end of the body. The simplest correct way: restore the file to its HEAD content (`git show HEAD:cocoder/tickets/open/0023-archive-priority-play-no-out-of-run-dispatch.md`) at the open/ path, and ensure no stray closed/ copy remains.
   - Restore `cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md` to its HEAD state (remove the 0023 closed-row you added). Do NOT otherwise modify INDEX.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (the ONLY net change vs HEAD must be the priority file move):
- `git status --porcelain` shows exactly: deleted `cocoder/priorities/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md` and untracked `cocoder/priorities/archive/orchestration-pipeline-simplification.md` (plus pre-existing unrelated churn like `cocoder/counters.json` / `cocoder/runs/**` which you did NOT touch).
- `cocoder/tickets/open/0023-archive-priority-play-no-out-of-run-dispatch.md` exists and `git diff HEAD -- cocoder/tickets/` is EMPTY (ticket tree byte-identical to HEAD).
- `order.json` valid JSON, unchanged.
- Archived priority file parses (frontmatter intact).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not edit any other priority or ticket, do not touch `cocoder/zArchive/`, do not run git commit. Report `git status --porcelain` and the result of `git diff HEAD -- cocoder/tickets/` (must be empty) in your summary.

WRITE SCOPE: cocoder/priorities/**, cocoder/tickets/**.

Atom 1 — Architecture linearization (DOCS ONLY). Produce the single canonical current-state orchestration map by EXTENDING `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Do NOT create a new doc and do NOT duplicate content the ADR index or owner map already own.

CONTEXT (read these first, do not restate them):
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §'How work reaches trunk — the commit spine (ADR-0023)' (around line 60) ALREADY linearizes the commit-spine lineage to current state.
- `cocoder/decisions/README.md` ALREADY owns ADR status and marks the superseded set ('Retired to history': 0015/0021/0022→0023, 0020-addendum→0026; merged 0009→0008, 0011→0013) and has a 'Reading order'. It is the ADR-status owner.
- `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` ALREADY owns the contract guarded-distinction table and verdicts. It is the contract drill-down owner.
The DEFICIT this atom fixes: there is no single current-state narrative for the orchestration/observation run loop. The persona/observation lineage (0013→0016→0017→0026) is scattered, and the 'Oz vs Debugger' section uses stale 'CoBuilder ORCH DEBUGGER' framing instead of the current model.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Add ONE new section to `ARCHITECTURE.md` (symmetric to the commit-spine section), e.g. '## How a run executes — the orchestration loop (ADR-0013 lineage)'. In plain current-state prose, describe how a run works NOW so an agent answers 'how does launch / orchestration / observation / repair work' from this one place WITHOUT chasing superseded ADRs:
   - Oscar drives Bob through a multi-atom plan: scope atom → delegate → verify gate (evidence before the spine commits; the old verify-gate ADR-0011 is folded into 0013) → next-or-wrap. (ADR-0013, current.)
   - Tiered observation: Oscar→Bob, Deb→Oscar, Oz→sessions; the 'direct your primary' rule. (ADR-0013.)
   - Deb is the scoped in-run repair fallback (live status feed + nudge channel + gate-enforced repair; advises Oscar, never rescues the run); repairs land via the commit spine. (ADR-0016, current.)
   - Oz is the idle, cross-session control-plane orchestration persona with a bounded daemon tool surface; Oz repair never touches a live Bob. (ADR-0017, current.)
   - Existing-repo onboarding/drift run as ordinary Oscar priorities reusing audit Plays as tooling — NOT a standalone phase-executor (that executor was retired). (ADR-0026 supersedes the 0020-addendum executor.)
   - Open the section with one line stating that the superseded predecessors in this lineage are retired (the 0020-addendum phase-executor) and point to `cocoder/decisions/README.md` for ADR status — DERIVE from it, do not re-list every ADR. Cross-link `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` for the per-contract owner map. Cross-link the existing commit-spine section for landing.
2. Lightly tighten the existing commit-spine section ONLY if needed so its lineage is explicit: it should make clear an agent reads ADR-0023 (+ its amendments 0024 governance-dirt self-heal and 0025 atomic authoring Plays) as current truth and does NOT need 0015/0021/0022 (retired). Add the 0025 pointer if absent. Keep edits minimal — do not rewrite the section.
3. Replace/fold the stale 'Oz vs Debugger' section (line ~238, 'CoBuilder ORCH DEBUGGER') so it reflects the current model and points to the new orchestration-loop section instead of repeating it. Remove the stale CoBuilder framing. Do not duplicate the new section's content — keep Oz-vs-Deb here as the boundary distinction only, or merge it into the new section and leave a pointer.
4. Confirm `cocoder/decisions/README.md` marks every superseded ADR in BOTH named lineages (commit-spine 0015→0021→0022→0023→0024→0025; persona/observation 0013→0016→0017→0026). I have verified it already does (0015/0021/0022 retired→0023; 0020-addendum→0026; 0011 merged→0013; 0024/0025/0016/0017/0026 are Accepted/current — NOT superseded). So make NO change unless you find a genuine missing supersede marker; if the index is already complete, do not touch it (note that in your summary).

HARD ANTI-DUPLICATION ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (this priority's whole point is FEWER concepts, not a new lane):
- No new doc file is created. Only `ARCHITECTURE.md` changes (and `cocoder/decisions/README.md` only if a real gap exists).
- The new section does NOT restate the founder-closeout label contract (owned by `wrap-up.md`/owner map), does NOT copy the guarded-distinction verdicts (owned by the owner map), and does NOT re-explain each ADR's full content (owned by decisions/README). It is a current-state narrative with pointers.
- An agent can answer 'how does commit / launch / orchestration / observation / repair / authoring work' from ARCHITECTURE.md, each anchored to its ONE current ADR, with superseded predecessors named as history and pointed (not chased).
- Net effect reduces chasing: prose is concise; no section is left contradicting the new one (especially the old Oz-vs-Debugger framing).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not edit personas, Plays, runner/daemon code, or tests in this atom (docs only). Do not run git or commit. Report the exact ARCHITECTURE.md sections added/changed and whether decisions/README needed any change.

WRITE SCOPE: ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/**, cocoder/decisions/**.

Atom 2 — Behavior-pinning test net for the PLAY TAXONOMY (TESTS ONLY, green on current main). This is the safety net that must exist BEFORE the one bounded refactor collapses the Play taxonomy. Extend the EXISTING suites; do NOT create a parallel harness file.

WHY: This priority will collapse the highest-confidence over-modeled concept, with the Play taxonomy (trigger class × execution model × write-authority; five named classes per ADR-0010's 2026-06-19 amendment) as the first candidate. Before that, the taxonomy's REAL observable contract must be captured as black-box tests so a later refactor that preserves behavior keeps them green and one that breaks behavior fails them.

FIRST, SURVEY (report findings in your summary, no separate doc): read the existing Play tests — `packages/core/tests/plays.test.ts`, `plays-triggers.test.ts`, `plays-request.test.ts`, `plays-dispatch.test.ts`, and the Play rows in `packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts` — and the owners `packages/core/src/plays/types.ts`, `triggers.ts`, `request.ts`, `dispatch.ts`, `manifest.ts`. State which of the four contract behaviors below are ALREADY black-box pinned (cite the test) vs only implementation-coupled or unpinned. Only ADD tests for genuinely unpinned/weakly-pinned behaviors — do not duplicate an existing equivalent assertion.

PIN THESE OBSERVABLE BEHAVIORS as black-box tests (assert on inputs→outputs/effects, NOT on the current internal class structure, so they survive a taxonomy collapse that preserves behavior):
1. Trigger-class boundary: a mandatory lifecycle/policy Play (e.g. the `run-wrap`→wrap-up binding) CANNOT be dispatched through the persona-requested lane — `request.ts` validation rejects it; and a persona-requested optional Play IS accepted when caller+id+input+scope are valid.
2. Optional-request validation: rejects unknown Play id, unauthorized caller, missing required input per inputSchema, and an out-of-bounds returned write scope; accepts a well-formed request.
3. Execution model: a prompt-only Play runs its body through the model; a hybrid Play runs its deterministic precheck and a NON-ZERO precheck exit GATES the Play (no model/LLM invocation), while a zero exit feeds output into the prompt. (Mirror the real dispatch path; reuse the style of `scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs` if helpful, but as a test assertion.)
4. Write-authority: a Play's declared `writeScope` is the boundary actually enforced when its changes are committed (a path outside writeScope is flagged/refused, in-scope paths commit) — pin at the observable commit-result level.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All new/changed tests live in existing suites under `packages/core/tests/**` (and/or extend `scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs` if a red→green proof fits) — NO new parallel harness file/dir.
- Tests are genuine black-box behavioral assertions, not tautologies and not assertions on the current 5-class internal shape.
- The full relevant suite is GREEN on current main. Run it and paste the actual command + exit status + pass counts in your summary (e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` or the repo's documented test command — discover it from package.json/CLAUDE/AGENTS, do not guess).
- NO production/source code changes (this atom is tests only). If a behavior cannot be pinned black-box without a source change, STOP and report it rather than editing source.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not modify `packages/core/src/**` or any persona/Play body; do not run git or commit. Report the survey findings, the exact tests added, and the green run evidence.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/tests/**, scripts/proof-orchestration-enforcer.mjs.

Atom 3 — Load-bearing verdicts (DOCS ONLY). For EACH guarded distinction in the predecessor owner map, give a one-line, evidence-backed verdict: real or suspect. EXTEND `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` with a new section '## Load-Bearing Verdicts (run_166)'. Do not create a new doc.

DEFINITIONS (from the priority Objective, requirement #3):
- real = collapsing the distinction would LOSE behavior, evidence, reversibility, or a safeguard → KEEP.
- suspect = NO safeguard/behavior/evidence is lost by collapsing → candidate to collapse (via a new founder-approved ADR).
CRITICAL RULE: the verdict is EVIDENCE-BACKED, not assumed-from-'an-ADR-exists'. An Accepted ADR documenting a distinction is NOT by itself proof it is load-bearing — you must say WHAT concrete behavior/safeguard would be lost on collapse (cite the code/test/runtime effect), or state that none would be. 'real' must name the specific thing lost; 'suspect' must say why nothing is lost.

INPUT — the guarded distinctions to verdict (from the owner map's run_164 table, `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md:204-216` and dispositions `:233-245`):
1. Priority launch vs ticket-fix launch vs ad-hoc support launch target handling.
2. Mandatory lifecycle Play triggers vs optional Play requests vs persona-authored Play-like behavior.
3. Base persona/Play governance (`packages/personas/base/**`) vs workspace governance (`cocoder/**`).
4. Wrap-up vs support-commit vs Deb repair vs ordinary verify-gated atom commits (four landing callers of the one spine).
5. Ticket/priorities authoring through daemon routes vs authoring Plays vs direct persona edits.
6. Drift/onboarding phase libraries vs ordinary Oscar priorities vs the retired phase-executor.
7. Founder closeout format vs visible closeout delivery.
8. Direct daemon priority/ticket routes vs authoring Plays.
9. Oz repair vs Deb repair.
10. Ticket launch auto-close vs archive-priority Play.
11. Governance pre-run snapshot vs ordinary governance authoring.

SPECIAL DEPTH — the PLAY TAXONOMY (this is requirement #4's first collapse candidate, so analyze it most rigorously, as its own subsection before the table): the taxonomy is trigger class × execution model × write-authority, with five named classes (ADR-0010's 2026-06-19 amendment). Do this concretely:
  a. ENUMERATE the actual shipped Play set (`packages/personas/base/plays/**` plus any `cocoder/plays/deltas/**`) — list each Play with its triggerClass, executionModel, and writeScope.
  b. Build a small matrix of which of the three axes / five classes are ACTUALLY exercised by that real set vs modeled-but-unused.
  c. Verdict each axis: is trigger class load-bearing? is execution model (prompt-only vs hybrid) load-bearing? is write-authority load-bearing? Cite the behavior-pinning tests just landed (`packages/core/tests/plays-request.test.ts`, `plays-dispatch.test.ts`, `plays-triggers.test.ts`) as the evidence of what observable behavior each axis controls — so the verdict reasons 'if these pinned behaviors are preserved, does the axis/class still carry weight?'.
  d. Conclude real or suspect for the taxonomy as a whole, and if suspect, name the SPECIFIC collapse (e.g. drop an unused axis, or merge classes that share behavior) and what the new ADR would need to say.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every one of the 11 distinctions above has a real|suspect verdict with a one-line concrete evidence basis (file/test/runtime effect), and NONE rests on 'an ADR exists' alone.
- The Play taxonomy subsection includes the enumerated real Play set, the axis/class exercise matrix, a per-axis verdict, and an overall real|suspect call with a concrete collapse proposal if suspect.
- Every distinction judged 'suspect' names a concrete, sequenced follow-up (which one is the single best collapse candidate this priority should do next, and the rest as named follow-ups — requirement #5: no unowned intentions).
- DOCS ONLY: only `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` changes. No source/test/persona edits, no new doc file, no ticket files in this atom (name follow-ups inline; ticket creation is a later step). Do not run git or commit.

Report: the section added, the taxonomy verdict (real/suspect) with its one-line basis, and which single distinction you recommend as the next collapse.

WRITE SCOPE: docs/**.
